page: Microsoft Azure E64pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Epdsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores powered by the Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor architecture operating at 3.4 GHz. The CPU subsystem includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache per core, and a shared 128 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a 3780 GB disk, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

In multi-core and memory-intensive benchmarks, the E64pds_v6 exhibits top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench multi-core evaluations. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 7269.04 GB/sec and uncached large block read speeds at 465.82 GB/sec. Database operations and key-value store performance are also notable, with Redis SET operations achieving 117.06 million operations per second and Passmark database operations ranking in the top tier. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category.

Given its ARM64 architecture and high resource density, the E64pds_v6 offers a highly cost-efficient profile for multi-threaded, memory-intensive workloads without the premium associated with traditional x86 architectures. The combination of 512 GB of RAM and 3780 GB of local storage makes it highly suitable for large-scale databases, Redis caching layers, high-throughput web serving, and software compilation tasks. It is less suited for workloads requiring specialized x86 instruction sets like AVX-512 or AMX, which are absent from this ARM processor.
description: The Microsoft Azure E64pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Epdsv6 family, featuring 64 dedicated Microsoft Neoverse-N2 physical cores running at 3.4 GHz. It is configured with 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio, and includes 3780 GB of local storage. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance delivers top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance, ranking in the top 10% for multi-threaded benchmarks, database operations, and Redis throughput. While single-core performance remains average, its massive memory capacity and local storage make it highly efficient for memory-optimized and storage-heavy workloads. It is best suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and parallel software compilation, offering a cost-effective ARM64 alternative to traditional x86 architectures.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 512 GB of RAM, and 3780 GB of local storage, optimized for high-throughput database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64pds_v6 is a 64-core ARM64 virtual machine with 512 GB RAM and 3780 GB local storage, built for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 virtual machine delivering high-density memory, local storage, and top-tier multi-core performance for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 Microsoft Neoverse-N2 cores running at 3.4 GHz
512 GB of RAM providing an 8.0 GB per core memory-optimized ratio
3780 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier multi-core, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Well-suited for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and web serving
